One Plus One is One

A Language Thought experiment on the Cosmological Singularity and the Big Bang

59 The Ultimate Technology is the Universe Itself

Tags: cloning differentiation in sameness principle of evolution
Normand Bourque Sun, Feb 2, 2014 6 min read

 

Terracotta Warriors, Xi’an, China

“I want to tell you that this matter of Who You Are and Who You Choose To Be, is of great importance. Not only because it sets the tone of your experience, but because it creates the Nature of Mine. All of your life you
have been told that God created you. I come now to tell you this: you are Creating God. That is a massive rearrangement of your understanding.” Conversations with God, Book 3

“Understand that you are the one for Me. I chose you, and I continue to choose you. You are My choice. I choose you for something, not for nothing. You are good use of My time. We serve each other. As I fill a need for you, I fulfill Myself. As you fill a need for Me, you fulfill Me – and yourself. Now, it’s a little tricky to say that you fulfill Me, for I am never in the slightest unfulfilled. Nevertheless, as I serve you, I am served. It is a kind of completion that I speak of. Something is accomplished. Something great is accomplished as We exchange our gifts.”  HL 4851

______________________

   [Note on the Terracotta warriors: 

   The Terracotta Army also called the “Terracotta Warriors and Horses” is a series of life-size sculptures representing the armies of the first emperor of China, Qin Shi Huang (246 BC) discovered in Xi’an, China. What is fascinating about those life-size sculptures (altogether over 7,000 pottery soldiers, horses, chariots and weapons) is that they were constructed on an assembly line of separate parts (head, arms, legs, and torso). For the face, eight molds were most likely used. Clay was then used to provide each soldier with a unique facial expression and positioned according to army rank (Wikipedia). It is a fascinating representation of the unique mold of Oneness to create embodied individualities (parts). Oneness experiencing Itself through Its parts. Creation in Wholeness, which is differentiation in sameness, is not cloning.]

   A massive rearrangement of our understanding of the Universe

Gradually, slowly yet but surely, we are letting go of the idea that technological science (including Internet and mobile devices), let go only to itself, IS evolution. If evolution is a process, technology is surely an attribute of this process as physical evolution but it is not the principle that leads that process. Even so, technology has been believed to be the cause of evolution since Darwin’s bible of the “Survival of the fittest.”

When we think that technology is the way to control evolution on earth (vaccines, genetic engineering, nuclear powering, aging and immortality control technology, etc.), we make a serious confusion between the principle of evolution and the process of evolution. Survival of the fittest means competition, elimination. However if we take a closer look at life, is it really about competing? If we believe that the process of evolution is competition, and its corollary — elimination or subordination of the weaker — does it make the idea of the fittest also the principle of evolution? If so, it would be equivalent to say that what produces evolution is evolution.

This is tautological, yet it is basically how we think when we put all our belief exclusively in the technological control of our planet and its inhabitants and, by extension, the surrounding universe. We ignore the real principle of evolution when we keep thinking that we are only observers of our own evolution and that we can manipulate it “from a distance” by external tools (including medical ones).

We are progressively becoming aware that, effectively, we can control the course of our own evolution, but not with a technology working on the survival of the fittest’s tautological process and principle.

The principle that underlies the process of evolution is that life is the common good that is shared not only by this planet but the whole universe. Everything that is “alive” — and everything in the universe is alive because everything vibrates — is contributing to life by the simple and fundamental fact that it is indeed alive. Life is essentially shared because it is One. And because it is One, it is indestructible and free and true. God would say: “I love every one of you. Each of you is perfect as I created you.”

Thus, the principle that underlies evolution is not the survival of the fittest, but Oneness expressing itself through us through the process of life in a multiplicity of ever-changing forms. If Oneness is the ultimate Truth, then it is the basic principle powering the process of evolution. Evolution is then the dynamic, ever-expanding expression of Truth. Hence all the technology on earth must aim at helping us to express the Truth of what we are. Competition is what we are not.

The technology of separation

It is a truism to say that our technology is based on separation. This, once again, is because we erroneously think that evolution is a process going on ‘outside’ of us (sources of energy, viruses and all forms of competing entities). In the meanwhile, advances in theoretical physics are bringing us closer to understanding our own mind which is mirrored in the nature of physicality itself.

Moreover, our technology is still mainly based on “combustion” and fossil energy rather than on electromagnetism. Our jumbo jets look like flying mammoths. So are our rockets launched in space. They seem to try painfully to go through air instead of simply sliding over it like flying saucers. Time travels, teleportation, ubiquity, the possibility to disassemble and reassemble our bodies to travel at light speed through space yet seem at the tip of our fingers but something is slipping off our hands at the same time.

This “missing link” in our technology is integration while we proceed technologically by separation (we still produce energy through nuclear fission rather than nuclear fusion). Our technology is still the expression of a huge and economically expensive effort against gravity while the Universe offers us a ready-made matrix that is a natural counterweight to gravity: anti-gravity.

   The Universe as a technological field or matrix

We well understand that opposites are necessary to enable us to re-create what we are by contrasting it with what we are not. This has been the long dualistic process of alternating war and peace periods at all levels of the human psyche.

However we have reached a point of no return in our arising awareness that all the possible opposites already exist in the Universe, and it is no longer necessary to use them or invent new ones to re-create the truth of what we really are. In other terms, we don’t need any more to be dualistic to define what we are. We don’t need any more negativity to see and use positivity by contrast. We can use positivity directly. To say that everything is ok is not a naïve statement. It is expressing the truth of what we are. All that we are not (dissimulation, lie, secrecy, darkness in general) is recorded in our memory (including our past and future lives) so that we don’t have to reenact them to define ourself as we are in truth. All the stories, all the scenarios ever to exist have already been written. They are engraved in our memory. This cosmological technological field from where we draw all that is necessary  to define what we want to be can be called the universal substance. Its principal or law is Truth, hence TRANSPARENCY.

Since time is an illusion, we can say that we have already experienced everything. We now just have to decide what we want to be without making a detour through what we are not, that is going directly to the Truth without any detour in dissimulation. This will have a huge impact on the way we define economic, social and technological policies…

 The universal technological key

As previously said, the major shift is a massive rearrangement of our understanding of the Universe. This can be accomplished only if we acknowledge the true principle that leads the process of evolution on earth and in the Universe.

Dualistic thinking establishes the thought sequence: have —> do —> be . “I need to HAVE this and that by Doing this and that in order to BE what I want to be”. This is unconscious evolution where human beings think that evolution or change is an outside force.

The universal technological field or “conscious evolution” is based on the thought sequence: Be —> Do —> Have. “Because I AM happy, I DO this and that and I know I HAVE everything because I AM EVERYTHING and EVERYBODY.”

As long as we will see competitivity in technology, it will be impossible to integrate the truthful thinking process of  Be—> Do —> Have. Technology is so motivated by competition that it has to dissimulate. So it functions along with the pattern of  Have —> Do —> Be. All our economic system is based on privacy and secrecy. Our justice system is based on punishment.

This is to say that our technology follows a dualistic process. That is why it is so costly and so polluting. It costs a society a lot to hide and compete.

If we want to know or “have” truth, justice, wealth, and happiness, we have to BE truth, Be justice, Be wealth, and Be happiness. The opposite of dualism is transparency.

Through transparency, we will know how to disassemble and reassemble bodies of our choice and be ubiquitous.

Written by Normand Bourque on Feb 02, 2014

59 The Ultimate Technology is the Universe Itself

Normand Bourque Sun, Feb 2, 2014 6 min read

A 360° VISION:

Because of our unconditional belief in scientific cosmology, notably in quantum physics, it seems that we are no longer sovereign of our spirit. Yet we need to expand our vision. We want a 360° vision, and that is an inner vision that prevents our outer vision from an excess of details that distract from reality. A thought experiment is a sinking into a reality where details are not present. It is a sinking process rather than an analyzing one.

A sample of chapter titles follow:

  • In the Beginning, there was the Present
  • Food for thoughts, thoughts for food
  • Have you ever tried counting to 1?
  • A particle has no parts
  • What is a singularity?
  • Is it really a Big Bang or an implosion?
  • The center of the initial singularity
